sprint: Identity Gate -- Inline Prove It's You Pattern Challenge
task: A real security/UX requirement: the boot-time Identity Gate ('Whose adventure is this?') let anyone continue as a known Creator, tap a picker tile, or pull in a different card, with zero proof of identity -- and the previous 'Recall a different card' option bounced all the way to Creator's own Screen 1 to show its tap grid there, explicitly the thing being fixed. Every path into an identity on the Gate (Continue My Journey, a picker tile tap, Recall a different card) now requires a real constellation-tap challenge, rendered inline in the same panel, never a navigation to a different screen. A new self-contained _renderPatternChallenge() component (its own --mc-* dark-navy/gold tap-and-connect board, a genuinely separate implementation from Creator's own module-private tap-grid helpers) gates proceed() behind either a local, order-independent pattern match (Continue/tile-tap, against the already-known local card) or the real cross-device MagicCard.recall() RPC (Recall a different card, with a typed-code fallback) -- one component, two verify functions. Begin Exploring stays deliberately ungated, since it grants no identity. The now-dead window.__magicCardAutoOpenRecall bridge (setter + consumer) is removed outright. Verified via a new 29-assertion Playwright suite plus full regression across the entire Companion/Magic-Card/Creator suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing (two pre-existing tests updated for the intentional new challenge step, one pre-existing ceremony flake confirmed unrelated via git stash). Confined to js/magicCardUI.js, js/creationFlow.js, and css/style.css.
